summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes XOR-encrypted Windows binary, then erases its own traces
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script `install-hook.js` invokes hidden PowerShell (base64/UTF-16LE encoded) which downloads `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin`, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, and launches it with `Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden`. After firing the dropper, the script performs anti-forensics: it walks up to the project root, removes the `mailconfirmer` entry from the host project's `package.json` and `package-lock.json`, kills a `dllhost.exe` watchdog, and schedules a batch file to `rmdir /s /q` its own `node_modules/mailconfirmer` directory so that the compromise cannot be traced back to this package. The advertised library API (`index.js`) is a no-op stub — `sendConfirmationEmail` only `console.log`s and `verifyToken` returns true — confirming the package exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the dropper. Name pattern (`mailconfirmer`) targets developers searching for email-confirmation utilities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
